text.title: Gcp Arch Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for the gcp-arch-patterns methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale + concrete remediation hook.

rule.statement: Production GKE clusters MUST be regional with private nodes, Workload Identity enabled, and managed Prometheus.
rule.rationale: Regional + private nodes + Workload Identity is the baseline that survives zone loss and avoids node-level SA keys.

rule.statement: Production Cloud SQL instances MUST set `availability_type=REGIONAL` and enable point-in-time recovery (PITR).
rule.rationale: REGIONAL with PITR is the only combination that survives a zonal outage AND lets us restore from arbitrary timestamps.

rule.statement: Static assets MUST be served from Cloud Storage behind a Global HTTPS Load Balancer with Cloud CDN enabled.
rule.rationale: Origin offload + edge caching dominates the cost / latency trade-off for static content.

rule.statement: Stateless microservices MUST default to Cloud Run; reach for GKE only if Cloud Run limits (memory, request length, networking) are exceeded.

rationale.source: Cloud Run vs GKE decision docs

rule.rationale: Cloud Run absorbs ops burden cheaper than GKE; reserving GKE for the cases that need it keeps the architecture lean.

rule.statement: Streaming + batch data pipelines MUST follow Pub/Sub → Dataflow → BigQuery topology with schemas registered in Schema Registry / Data Catalog.
rule.rationale: This is the canonical reference pipeline; following it leverages managed scaling and schema governance out of the box.

rule.statement: If the input fails ALL "Applies If" preconditions or matches ANY "Skip If" disqualifier, do not apply this methodology — route the task to the adjacent sibling instead.
rule.rationale: Even flat checklists need an explicit skip leaf in the decision tree; this rule is the universal terminal.
